channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: LA wildfires destroy numerous houses of worship. Clergy and congregants vow to persevere
item.description: Flames were already attacking the campus of Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center when the cantor, Ruth Berman Harris, and three companions rushed in to rescue its sacred Torah scrolls.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Powerful winter storm dumps heavy snow, causing flight delays, slick roads and school closings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma lumbered eastward into Southern U.S. states Friday, making for dicey travel and a rare snow day for many students.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: FBI must be independent and above the partisan fray, outgoing director says in farewell address
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI must remain independent, above the partisan fray and “committed to upholding the rule of law,” outgoing Director Christopher Wray said in a farewell address just days before his expected retirement at the conclusion of the Biden administration following more than seven year
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Disney pledges $15 million in Los Angeles fire aid as more stars learn they've lost their homes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Pacific Palisades wildfires torched the home of “This Is Us” star Milo Ventimiglia, perhaps most poignantly destroying the father-to-be's newly installed crib.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Winter storm lets Southern states enjoy a rare snow day
item.description: When life gives you a snow day, make a snowman. Across the U.S. South, millions of residents woke up Friday to a rare chance to bust out the mittens and snow pants thanks to a cold snap that dumped snow in their parks, streets and backyards.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, TRAVIS LOLLER and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 18 migrants found stowed in tanker truck near San Antonio
item.description: A group of 18 migrants were found inside a tanker truck by Texas law enforcement hundreds of miles from the border in Guadalupe County, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida woman messages investigator instead of drug dealer by mistake
item.description: A woman landed herself in hot water when she texted someone she believed was a drug dealer, but accidentally messaged a Bay County narcotics investigator instead.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, KRON4

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt for defaming Georgia election workers
item.description: A federal judge on Friday held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt for skirting an agreement not to make new false claims about two Georgia election workers he was ordered to pay $146 million for defaming.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass faces critical leadership test as questions emerge about wildfire response
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass is facing withering criticism for her response to the firestorm ravaging her city.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi And Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palisades Fire threatens Los Angeles' San Fernando Valley
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - The Palisades Fire, the most destructive wildfire in Los Angeles history, edged closer to one of the city's major population centers on Friday: the San Fernando Valley.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Trump vows to shed light on mystery drones when president
item.description: Trump, who will be inaugurated as the 47th president on Jan. 20, criticized the Biden administration's handling of the mystery drones.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors seek 15 years in prison for former New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez after bribery conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Prosecutors say former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez should be imprisoned for 15 years for a “grave abuse of his power,” after the New Jersey Democrat became the first person to be convicted of abusing a Senate committee leadership position and the first U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DHS extends TPS for Salvadorans, potentially complicating Trump's plans
item.description: The Biden administration on Friday extended a key humanitarian program for El Salvadoran nationals in a move that could complicate the incoming Trump administration’s plans to do away with long-term immigrant protections.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Trump assassination attempt victims 'honored' to attend 47th president's inauguration
item.description: Trump assassination attempt victims James Copenhaver and David Dutch, who were injured in the July shooting in Pennsylvania, will be attending the 47th president's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg returns to his wildfire-charred LA neighborhood
item.description: Steve Guttenberg awoke Thursday morning to a grim reality: The treacherous wildfire that tore through the Pacific Palisades had left his once-lush neighborhood charred and unrecognizable.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Civilian drone hits, damages firefighting aircraft over Palisades Fire
item.description: A firefighting aircraft that was operating over the Palisades Fire is grounded and out of service after it was struck and damaged by a civilian’s drone on Thursday afternoon, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Man detained near Kenneth Fire not accused of arson
item.description: Editor's Note: This article has been updated and a video removed to clarify there were no arson charges. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - A man who was detained by citizens shortly after a wildfire exploded near Calabasas and West Hills is not accused of starting the fire, authorities announced Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KRON4

item.title: Man detained near Kenneth Fire not accused of arson
item.description: Editor's Note: This article has been updated and a video removed to clarify there were no arson charges. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - A man who was detained by citizens shortly after a wildfire exploded near Calabasas and West Hills is not accused of starting the fire, authorities announced Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to be sentenced in New York hush money case
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court rejected a last-ditch effort by lawyers for President-elect Donald Trump to prevent his sentencing for his New York criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, KRON4

item.title: Bourbon Street terror victims sue New Orleans as Louisiana AG investigates security lapses
item.description: At least two dozen New Orleans terrorist attack victims are filing lawsuits against the city and its police department, according to the law firms representing them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: What ignited the deadly California wildfires? Investigators consider an array of possibilities
item.description: Investigators are considering an array of possible ignition sources for the huge fires that have killed at least 10 people and destroyed thousands of homes and businesses in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Jason Dearen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New LA-area fire prompts more evacuations while over 10,000 structures lost to the 2 biggest blazes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The two biggest wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area have burned at least 10,000 homes, buildings and other structures, officials said Thursday as they urged more people to heed evacuation orders after a new blaze ignited and quickly grew.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A powerful winter storm's heavy snow and sleet cancels flights and closes schools across US South
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A powerful winter storm brought heavy snow and icy conditions to parts of the U.S. South on Thursday, forcing officials to close schools, cancel flights and warn residents in some of the worst-hit areas to stay off roads as it lumbered eastward through Oklahoma and Texas. Arkansas Gov.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Mexico is a go-to state for women seeking abortions. A new court ruling helps it stay that way
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — The New Mexico Supreme Court on Thursday struck down abortion restrictions by conservative cities and counties, helping to ensure the state remains a go-to destination for people from other states with bans.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LIVE: More evacuations ordered after fast-moving Kenneth fire ignites in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters are racing to contain a new wildfire that broke out close to homes in the area of Calabasas and Hidden Hills, northwest of Los Angeles, on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KRON4

item.title: Judge scraps Biden's Title IX rules, reversing expansion of protections for LGBTQ+ students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration’s Title IX rules expanding protections for LGBTQ+ students have been struck down nationwide after a federal judge in Kentucky found they overstepped the president’s authority. In a decision issued Thursday, U.S. District Judge Danny C.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lisa Kudrow says Matthew Perry left a note for her inside a recognizable 'Friends' prop
item.description: "I hadn't opened it up or looked inside it, but yeah he did. He had a note in there and I forgot about it," Kudrow said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, KRON4

item.title: Utah firefighters answer the call to battle Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: After a quick briefing, they hit the road to begin a 12-hour drive to California to battle the deadly and devastating Palisades Fire in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia mother shocked by burial of murdered daughter
item.description: Saunders sent photos of holes in the dirt on the gravesite, and one of them exposed the corner of her daughter's purple casket.
item.dc:creator: Chris Horne, NewsNation

item.title: All 5 living presidents, political dignitaries attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Political leaders gathered to honor the life of former President Jimmy Carter during his state funeral at the Washington National Cathedral on Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: How to safely evacuate a wildfire
item.description: Evacuating a wildfire area can be scary, but experts say people can significantly improve their own safety with a little preparation and common sense.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NY top court denies delaying Trump sentencing
item.description: New York’s high court on Thursday declined to halt President-elect Trump’s sentencing on his hush money criminal conviction, which is scheduled for Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Celebrity homes among those lost in devastating Los Angeles fires
item.description: Wildfires that are burning in and around Los Angeles have burned the homes of several celebrities, including Billy Crystal, Mandy Moore and Paris Hilton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hollywood Hills fire breaks out as deadly wildfires burn out of control across Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A fast-moving fire broke out in the Hollywood Hills on Wednesday night, threatening one of Los Angeles' most iconic spots as firefighters battled to get under control three other major blazes that killed five people, put 130,000 people under evacuation orders and ravaged communiti
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4 fraternity members at San Diego State University charged after one was set on fire during a 'skit': DA
item.description: The incident occurred last February, when the fraternity was on probation with the university over issues of hazing and conduct that threatens the health and safety of students.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, ABC 4

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KTLA 5
